title: Cracked phone screen — DIY first response

steps.visual_prompt_start: The same cracked phone screen as the reference image, lying flat on a clean white workbench under bright neutral lighting, captured head-on.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Same cracked phone on the same workbench, a fingertip enters from the right side of the frame and points precisely at the longest crack line on the screen.
steps.motion_prompt: A fingertip slowly enters from the right and points at the longest crack on the phone screen, no other motion.
steps.narration: Start by inspecting the damage. Identify the longest cracks and gently tap different zones to check whether the touch layer underneath is still responsive.
steps.subtitle: Inspect cracks and check touch response.

steps.visual_prompt_start: Same cracked phone on the same workbench, thumb hovering just above the right-side power button of the phone, the screen still showing the cracks but is on.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Same cracked phone with the 'slide to power off' interface displayed across the screen, the slider visible behind the cracks.
steps.motion_prompt: A thumb presses and holds the side power button of the cracked phone, the screen content transitions to show the slide-to-power-off interface.
steps.narration: Power down the phone before attempting any repair. Hold the side button until the power slider appears, then slide right to fully shut it off.
steps.subtitle: Power the phone off before any repair.

steps.visual_prompt_start: Same powered-off cracked phone on the workbench, a small green suction cup with a handle placed next to the bottom edge of the phone, both clearly visible.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Same cracked phone with the green suction cup now firmly attached to the bottom-center of the cracked screen, two fingers gripping the suction cup handle ready to pull.
steps.motion_prompt: Two hands pick up the green suction cup, press it firmly against the bottom edge of the cracked phone screen, then grip the handle preparing to pull upward.
steps.narration: Attach a small suction cup near the bottom edge. Press it down firmly to create a tight seal, then pull straight up to gently lift the broken screen panel.
steps.subtitle: Suction cup at the bottom edge to lift the screen.
